source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the diagram, given that the area of the square $ABCD$ is 64 square centimeters and $DE = 10$ cm, what is the length of $CE$?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text: \textbf{Solution:} Since the area of square ABCD is 64 square centimeters, $\therefore$ $\angle ADC=90^\circ$ and $DC=8$ cm. Since $DE=10$ cm, $\therefore$ $CE=\sqrt{DE^{2}+DC^{2}}=\sqrt{8^{2}+10^{2}}=\boxed{2\sqrt{41}}$ (cm).

multi_step: solution uses at least two reasoning steps: derive square side length from area 64, then apply a right-triangle length formula
problem combines textual givens (area, DE=10) with diagram-based geometry
answer_text indicates intermediate inference before final computation
verifiability: final answer is a specific closed-form value: 2ŌłÜ41 cm
the derivation is checkable from stated quantities and standard geometry rules
single numeric result makes downstream verification straightforward
